title: Algerian Pork Tenderloin Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic North African-inspired dish made with tenderloin, spices, and dried fruits. Serve with couscous or rice.
instructions: Season the pork tenderloin with Algerian Spice Blend, salt, and black pepper.
Heat coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork tenderloin until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the pork to a plate and set aside.
Add onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add ginger and garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add dried apricots and pitted dates to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Return the pork tenderloin to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the pork reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Remove the pot from the oven and let the pork rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve the pork with the sauce spooned over the top.
